text.title: Review Comments and Agent Workflow
text.summary: Conventional Comments label system, good/bad comment examples, reviewer/author rules, and agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Every review comment must carry one of: blocking, suggestion, nit, question, or praise. No unlabeled comments.
rule.rationale: Authors cannot distinguish a blocker from a style preference without explicit labels; unlabeled comments cause PR stalls.

list.item: blocking: must fix before merge — bugs, security issues, standard violations.
suggestion: should consider — improvements that would make code better but are not required.
nit: minor style or preference — mark explicitly as non-blocking.
question: reviewer does not understand — ask before flagging as a problem.
praise: acknowledge good code — required, not optional.

rule.statement: Every blocking comment must quote the offending lines and provide a concrete suggested fix or explain the exact failure scenario.
rule.rationale: Vague blocking comments ("this is wrong") waste author time and create friction; specific comments with fixes are immediately actionable.

example.note: Vague: no explanation, no fix, no quoted line.
Specific: quotes the exact line, explains the failure scenario, provides the fix.
example.code:
blocking: Line 47 will raise KeyError when `user` is None because `user['id']` is accessed unconditionally. Add a guard:
if not user: return None

list.item: Review within 4 hours; never delay more than 24 hours.
Approve when code is good enough, not only when perfect.
Do not block on nits; mark them and approve.
Do not rewrite the author's code in comments; suggest, don't replace.
Always include at least one praise comment per PR.
Self-review the diff before requesting review.
Respond to every comment, even if only "Done" or "Won't fix — see reason."
Never submit untested or uncompiled code for review.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated bugs: agent flags an NPE on code that already null-checks.
antipattern.reason: Require the agent to cite the exact line and explain the concrete failing input; reject blocking comments that cannot demonstrate the failure.

antipattern.description: Context truncation: agent reviews files 1-3 fully, rubber-stamps files 4-12 of a large PR.
antipattern.reason: Force the agent to enumerate files reviewed; if it had to skip any, it must refuse to approve and ask for a PR split.

antipattern.description: Self-review bias: agent reviewing a Claude-authored diff tends to agree with itself.
antipattern.reason: Always require a human review pass after agent review on agent-authored code.

rule.statement: The agent is never the sole approver for a merge to main; branch protection must require a human reviewer regardless of bot approval.
rule.rationale: Autonomous approval removes the human quality gate; even a high-precision agent will miss intent-level errors.

text.reference: Fetch PR metadata and diff for the review agent.
Agent review prompt with Conventional Comments constraints.
